commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a shared Codex/Harmony artifact detector and skips text-only artifact reply payloads before channel delivery.

PR surface: Source +32, Tests +74. Total +106 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Mantis Telegram Desktop proof shows current main sending `<channel|>` as a visible bot message and the candidate no longer showing that marker for the same probe.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainer review should decide whether the shared suppression tradeoff is acceptable; no automated code repair is needed if accepted.

target.title: fix(delivery): suppress Codex/Harmony internal protocol artifacts from user-facing channels
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #88128 — Internal messages (`<channel|>`, `set-thought <channel|>`, `───`) surface in Telegram chat as standalone visible messages.

**Root cause:** Codex/Harmony providers emit internal protocol fragments (channel routing markers, reasoning lane directives, box-drawing HRs from the internal markdown renderer) as standalone text deltas during streaming. The shared `normalizeReplyPayload` pipeline had no guard for these artifacts, so they passed through to all messaging channels as deliverable payloads.

**Fix:** Adds a narrow `isInternalFormattingArtifact()` detector in `src/auto-reply/tokens.ts` that catches only observed protocol-only markers, then short-circuits delivery in `normalizeReplyPayload` with a new `"internalArtifact"` skip reason.

## Design decisions (vs #88194)

This PR takes a **narrower approach** than the existing #88194, addressing ClawSweeper's P1 findings:

| Concern | #88194 (broad) | This PR (narrow) |
|---------|---------------|-----------------|
| Generic `---`, `***`, `___` | Suppressed (false-positive risk) | **Not suppressed** — legitimate markdown |
| Generic `<tag>`, `</tag>` | Suppressed (false-positive risk) | **Not suppressed** — could be user content |
| Harmony `<word\|value>` markers | Suppressed | Suppressed |
| `set-thought` directives | Suppressed | Suppressed |
| Box-drawing `───` (renderer HR) | Suppressed | Suppressed (unambiguous internal artifact) |

The two regexes are anchored (`^...$`) and require the artifact to be the **entire message content** — text that merely *contains* an artifact pattern (e.g. "Use `<channel|>` in your config") is never suppressed.

## Test plan

- [x] `src/auto-reply/tokens.test.ts` — 8 new test cases covering matches and false-positive rejections
- [x] `src/auto-reply/reply/reply-utils.test.ts` — integration test for `normalizeReplyPayload` skip reason
- [x] All 111 tests pass locally (`pnpm test -- src/auto-reply/tokens.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-utils.test.ts`)
- [x] Type-check passes cleanly (`pnpm tsgo`)

## Files changed

- `src/auto-reply/tokens.ts` — new `isInternalFormattingArtifact()` export
- `src/auto-reply/reply/normalize-reply.ts` — artifact skip before `sanitizeUserFacingText`
- `src/auto-reply/tokens.test.ts` — unit tests for the detector
- `src/auto-reply/reply/reply-utils.test.ts` — integration test for skip reason

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: #88128 — Internal protocol fragments (`<channel|>`, `set-thought <channel|>`, box-drawing `───`) from Codex/Harmony providers are delivered as visible standalone messages to Telegram users during streaming.
- Real environment tested: macOS 15, Node 22, OpenClaw dev build from this branch, Telegram bot channel connected locally with gateway running in local mode.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Sent a message via Telegram to the bot running the patched build with a Codex provider configured. Observed the streaming response. Also ran `npx tsx -e "const {isInternalFormattingArtifact} = await import('./src/auto-reply/tokens.js'); console.log(isInternalFormattingArtifact('<channel|>'));console.log(isInternalFormattingArtifact('set-thought <thinking|>'));console.log(isInternalFormattingArtifact('───────'));console.log(isInternalFormattingArtifact('Hello world'));"` to verify the detector against real artifact strings copied from #88128 issue report.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Detector output: `<channel|>` = true, `set-thought <thinking|>` = true, `───────` = true, `Hello world` = false. No internal artifacts appeared in the Telegram chat after the fix. Previously these would appear as separate visible messages between real response chunks.
- Observed result after fix: Internal protocol artifacts are now filtered before delivery. The `normalizeReplyPayload` function returns skip reason `"internalArtifact"` for these patterns, preventing them from reaching any messaging channel. Normal user-facing content passes through unchanged.
- What was not tested: Discord, Slack, and other channels (the fix is in the shared `normalizeReplyPayload` path so all channels benefit). WhatsApp Web streaming behavior with these specific providers.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The Codex/Harmony provider artifacts are provider-specific streaming behavior that occurs intermittently during long responses. The detector patterns were derived from artifacts reported in #88128 and confirmed in live Telegram sessions.

---

[Joel Nishanth](https://offlyn.ai) | [offlyn.AI](https://offlyn.ai)
target.author: joelnishanth
